steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Subtract $8$, then divide by $5$.
$$5x=50\;\Rightarrow\;x=10$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. It's given that the function $f$ is defined by $f\left(x\right)=5x+8$. Substituting $58$ for $f\left(x\right)$ in this equation yields $58=5x+8$. Subtracting $8$ from both sides of this equation yields $50=5x$. Dividing both sides of this equation by $5$ yields $10=x$. Therefore, the value of $x$ when $f\left(x\right)=58$ is $10$.

steps.content: Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $f\left(58\right)$, not the value of $x$ when $f\left(x\right)=58$.
